title: CBP Agents Kill U.S. Citizen Alex Pretti in Minneapolis; Videos Contradict DHS Account
excerpt: Border Patrol agents fatally shot Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse and U.S. citizen, on a Minneapolis street on January 24, 2026. Eyewitness video and a DHS report to Congress contradicted the administration's initial claim that Pretti was 'brandishing' a firearm.
content:
On January 24, 2026, CBP agents fatally shot Alex Jeffrey Pretti, a 37-year-old U.S. citizen and VA intensive care nurse, near the intersection of 26th Street and Nicollet Avenue in Minneapolis. The shooting occurred amid widespread protests against Operation Metro Surge following the January 7 killing of Renée Good.

Multiple videos of the encounter verified by Reuters, the BBC, The Wall Street Journal, and NBC News showed Pretti holding only a phone and interposing himself between an agent and a woman who had been pushed. He was tackled, pepper-sprayed, and disarmed before being shot. DHS officials initially called him a 'domestic terrorist' who sought to 'massacre law enforcement.' The White House later distanced itself from this account, acknowledging agents may not have followed protocol.

  • A DHS report to Congress confirmed two agents discharged their weapons. The report did not state Pretti attempted to reach for a weapon before being shot.
  • Border Patrol on-site commander Gregory Bovino was subsequently stripped of his 'commander-at-large' title and removed from Minnesota.
  • DHS blocked Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension investigators from the scene, even after the state obtained a search warrant.
  • The DOJ opened a civil rights investigation into Pretti's death on January 30, 2026.
"Two 37-year-old Minnesotans are now dead, a poet and a nurse, for what?" — Rep. Kelly Morrison (D-MN)
mediaPlaceholder: Eyewitness video of Alex Pretti's encounter with and fatal shooting by CBP agents
